auth.login_required: Please log in to submit IOCs

search.title: Search & Investigate
search.placeholder: Search text or pick a line in the list (country or type with count)…
search.browse_label: Browse by country or IOC type (counts)

bulk.title: Submit IOCs
bulk.comment_placeholder: Comment to apply to all findings...
bulk.paste_placeholder: Paste text here... IPs, domains, URLs, emails, hashes will be extracted on Preview.
bulk.info_paste_desc: Paste any text (logs, reports, emails). Click Preview to extract IPs, domains, URLs, emails, and hashes (MD5/SHA1/SHA256) into the table below.
bulk.info_single_desc: Submit one IOC at a time. Fill value, type, analyst, optional comment and TTL. Preview then Approve All.
bulk.info_txt_desc: Upload raw log files or IOC lists. Parser detects IOCs automatically. Format: IOC_VALUE # Metadata. Extracts Ticket ID, Analyst, Timestamps.
bulk.info_csv_desc: Standard CSV. Scans every cell for IOCs. Auto-detects Ticket ID / Report ID columns. Ideal for bulk report ingestion.
bulk.already_exists: This IOC already exists in the system. Approve is disabled.

yara.title: YARA Manager
yara.my_pending_hint: These rules will appear in the feed after an admin approves them.
yara.info_upload_desc: Choose a .yar file, add optional ticket ID, comment, and campaign. Run Check syntax until it passes-Submit stays disabled until the file compiles. The rule is submitted for approval before it appears in the active repository.
yara.info_write_title: ✏️ Write a rule in the browser
yara.info_write_desc: Paste or type YARA source (live syntax colors on the right), set the filename (must end in .yar), and run Check syntax until it passes-Submit stays disabled until the current source compiles (same compiler check as Upload). Same approval flow as file upload.
yara.info_status_desc: Review rules awaiting approval and browse approved rules in the repository. Filter the active list by name, comment, analyst, or ticket.
yara.submit_requires_valid_syntax: Run Check syntax successfully on the current rule source before submitting.
yara.submit_blocked_toast: Cannot submit-the rule is not valid yet. Use Check syntax and fix errors until you see the green Syntax OK message.
yara.admin_delete_hint: A reason is required. It appears in Search → History for this filename and in audit logs. Automation targets receive an HTTP DELETE if configured.
yara.admin_delete_placeholder: Why is this rule being removed?
yara.syntax_required_hint: Run Check syntax first-the rule must compile successfully before Submit is enabled.
yara.filter_placeholder: Filter rules by name, comment, analyst, or ticket...

champs.title: Champs Analysis
champs.spotlight_click: Click an analyst to view details
champs.msg_settings_hint: Up to 5 messages. They will loop with a green separator. All text is bold.

campaign.title: Campaign Graph
campaign.name: Name
campaign.delete_confirm_message: Linked IOCs will be unlinked (not deleted).
campaign.ref_image_hint: Screenshot or email excerpt (JPG, PNG, GIF, WebP, max 8 MB).
campaign.graph_banner_inactive: Inactive for blocking feeds / Live Stats: no IOC with a valid (non-expired) date. Linked IOCs: {linked} total ({active} active, {expired} expired). YARA rules: {yara}.
campaign.graph_banner_inactive_yara_only: No active IOCs — this campaign does not appear in Campaign Impact. YARA rule(s) linked: {yara}.
campaign.graph_banner_inactive_empty: No IOCs or YARA linked to this campaign yet.

delete_modal.title: Delete IOC
delete_modal.message: You must provide a description of why this indicator is being deleted.
delete_modal.reason_placeholder: e.g. False positive, migrated to other system...
delete_modal.reason_required: Please provide a reason for deletion.

private_confirm.step1_message: The following indicator is in a private or internal range (e.g. RFC 1918, .local). Blocking it may affect internal systems. Are you sure you want to continue?
private_confirm.step2_message: If you proceed, this indicator will be submitted and may be deployed to blocking systems. Proceed?

modal.yara_edit_reason_label: Why did you change the rule?
modal.yara_edit_reason_placeholder: Required when you change the rule text (shown in Search & Investigate history)

toast.select_yara_file: Please select a YARA rule file first.
toast.yara_edit_reason_required: Describe why you changed the rule (required when the rule text changes)
toast.invalid_file_type: Invalid file type. Only .yar files are allowed.
toast.private_ip_warning: Contains private/internal IPs - blocking may cut internal access

history.empty: No history recorded for this IOC.

feedpulse.title: Feed Pulse
feedpulse.no_outgoing: No outgoing IOCs in this window.
feedpulse.no_incoming: No incoming IOCs in this window.
feedpulse.connections_feed_modal_sub: Clients that successfully pulled plain-text feeds (/feed/…) or TAXII 2 (/taxii2/…). Throttled telemetry per client IP and path.
feedpulse.pull_state_modal_sub: When ZIoCHub last connected outbound to another system to pull IOCs into the database (currently MISP).
feedpulse.push_state_modal_sub: When ZIoCHub last pushed or updated IOC/YARA in linked platforms (vendor APIs, HTTP automations, DXL/TIE, etc.).
feedpulse.connections_subtitle: Last activity from feed consumers and ZIoCHub integrations (no server log access required).
feedpulse.connections_pull_sub: Last time ZIoCHub connected to an external system to pull IOCs and update the database (MISP).
feedpulse.connections_push_state_sub: Last connection to update external systems from Admin → Integrations (Cortex XDR, Google SecOps). Host and resolved IP when available.
feedpulse.connections_push_state_empty: No vendor push integrations are configured.
feedpulse.connections_automation_sub: One row per admin-defined URL: display name, host (IP or domain), latest attempt time, and which push types touched this target.
feedpulse.connections_empty_automation: No outbound push targets recorded yet.
feedpulse.connections_last_yara_auto_push: Last YARA push to external targets (after approve)
feedpulse.connections_last_yara_auto_delete: Last YARA delete on external targets (after remove)
feedpulse.allowlist_hint: Content managed by Admin. Lines starting with # are comments.

feed_catalog.title: Available Feed Endpoints
feed_catalog.description: Use these URLs to integrate ZIoCHub feeds into your security devices. All feeds serve only active (non-expired) IOCs in plain text.
feed_catalog.standard_desc: Newline-separated list of IOC values. Compatible with most firewalls and SIEMs.
feed_catalog.paloalto_desc: External Dynamic List format. URLs are served without the http(s):// protocol prefix.
feed_catalog.checkpoint_desc: CSV format compatible with Check Point IoC feed import.
feed_catalog.cisco_esa_desc: Comma-separated email list for Cisco Email Security Appliance content filters.
feed_catalog.trellix_epo_desc: CSV hash feeds grouped by Ticket ID for Trellix Endpoint Security / ePO policy import.
feed_catalog.stix_desc: STIX 2.1 JSON bundle of indicators. Use with TAXII clients or any tool that consumes STIX format.
feed_catalog.stix_short: STIX 2.1 indicators via TAXII 2.1 or as JSON bundle. Any TAXII-capable device (e.g. Cisco IronPort ESA, ThreatConnect) can use the Discovery URL; HTTPS recommended.
feed_catalog.stix_ironport: For Cisco IronPort / ESA (External Threat Feed): set the TAXII server URL to this Discovery endpoint (HTTPS recommended).
feed_catalog.yara_desc: List and download approved YARA rule files for EDR / sandbox integration.

taxii_catalog.title: TAXII 2.1 Server Endpoints
taxii_catalog.description: ZIoCHub exposes active IOCs as STIX 2.1 indicators over TAXII 2.1. Use these URLs in TAXII clients (e.g. Cisco IronPort ESA, ThreatConnect). Send Accept: application/taxii+json;version=2.1.
taxii_catalog.ironport_hint: For TAXII clients (e.g. Cisco IronPort ESA): set the server or feed URL to the Discovery endpoint above. Use HTTPS in production.

playbook.title: Hunter's Playbook
playbook.delete_confirm_message: Are you sure you want to delete this item? This cannot be undone.

sanity.defanged: Defanged URL/domain detected. Use http/https and normal dots.
sanity.tld_only: Blocking entire TLD would break the internet. Block specific domains.
sanity.critical_infra: Critical infrastructure IP - blocking would break DNS.
sanity.url_hash: URL contains hash-like string (MD5/SHA1/SHA256). Verify correct.
sanity.hash_mismatch: Hash length: MD5=32, SHA1=40, SHA256=64. Verify type.
sanity.bogon_ip: Bogon/reserved IP range. Not routable on the public internet.
sanity.popular_domain: High-traffic domain. Blocking could disrupt critical services.
sanity.cloud_provider: Cloud/CDN shared infrastructure. Blocking may affect many legitimate services.
sanity.punycode_domain: Punycode/IDN domain. May be a homograph phishing attack.
sanity.dga_suspect: Domain has DGA-like characteristics. Possibly auto-generated by malware.
sanity.url_credentials: URL contains embedded credentials (user:pass@). Security risk.
sanity.url_raw_ip: URL uses raw IP instead of domain. Common in C2/malware.
sanity.deep_subdomain: Excessive subdomain depth. May indicate DNS tunneling or C2.
sanity.free_email_provider: Free email provider domain. Blocking would cut email access.
sanity.stale_ioc: Permanent IOC active for a long time. Consider reviewing or setting an expiration.

reports.title: Intelligence Reports
reports.select_report: Select a report from the left panel
reports.no_campaigns_created: No campaigns created in this period
